top_categories.workout_context: Boulder Roubaix Road Race's Packed dirt farm roads, Rutted unpaved county roads forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 30 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Boulder Roubaix Road Race's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
When someone attacks on rolling mixed-surface — unpaved county roads and, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Boulder Roubaix Road Race, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
2-minute all-out efforts on rolling mixed-surface — unpaved county roads and. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
30 miles on rolling mixed-surface — unpaved county roads and burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Boulder Roubaix Road Race's rolling mixed-surface — unpaved county roads and rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
At Boulder Roubaix Road Race, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Smooth pedaling at 30 miles saves muscle. Bad technique at mile 10 is invisible. At mile 80 it's a cramp.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Boulder Roubaix Road Race's rolling mixed-surface — unpaved county roads and actually does to you.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Boulder Roubaix Road Race. Train it until it feels boring.
Varied gradients on rolling mixed-surface — unpaved county roads and demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.

race_overlay.nutrition: Standard hydration and fueling. One bottle per hour minimum. Gel or bar every 45 minutes at race intensity.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Boulder Roubaix Road Race. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Boulder Roubaix Road Race requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on rolling mixed-surface — unpaved county roads and paved rural roads to build confidence. Expect: packed dirt farm roads, rutted unpaved county roads, paved rural roads.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 30 miles of rolling mixed-surface — unpaved county roads and paved rural roads in Boulder Reservoir, Boulder County, Colorado, USA.
